items.title: Eigenvector direction preserved under A
items.mermaid:
flowchart LR
  v0["eigenvector v"] -->|"multiply by A"| v1["lambda * v"]
  note["same line through origin"]
items.caption: Applying A to an eigenvector only scales it by λ; the direction (line through the origin) stays fixed.

items.title: Diagonalization as change of basis
items.mermaid:
flowchart LR
  x["x in standard basis"] --> P["P: eigenvector basis"]
  P --> d["D: scale by eigenvalues"]
  d --> Pinv["P inverse back"]
  Pinv --> Ax["A x"]
items.caption: A = P D P⁻¹ means: move to eigen-coordinates, scale by D, return to the original basis.
